perturbation.original_text: Global Energy represents and warrants that the statements contained in this Section 4 are true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and will be true and correct as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Global Energy represents and warrants that the statements contained in this Section 4 are believed to be true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and will be believed to be true and correct as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'believed to be' to the representation and warranties, the statement becomes subjective and less enforceable. This undermines the legal weight of the representations, making it difficult to hold Global Energy accountable for inaccuracies.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio Revised Code § 1701.95 places responsibility on corporate officers for the accuracy of representations. Including 'believed to be' weakens this obligation, allowing officers to potentially avoid liability for inaccurate representations if they claim they genuinely believed the statements were true, even if they lacked reasonable grounds for such belief. This contradicts the intent of the statute, which seeks to ensure due diligence and accurate disclosure.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Section 1701.95 - Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top The Legislative Service Commission staff updates the Revised Code on an ongoing basis, as it completes its act review of enacted legislation. Updates may be slower during some times of the year, depending on the volume of enacted legislation. Section 1701.95 | Liability for unlawful loans, dividends, distribution of assets. Ohio Revised Code / Title 17 Corporations-Partnerships / Chapter 1701 General Corporation Law Effective: July 6, 2001 Latest Legislation: Senate Bill 108 - 124th General Assembly PDF: Download Authenticated PDF (A)(1) In addition to any other liabilities imposed by law upon directors of a corporation and except as provided in division (B) of this section, directors shall be jointly and severally liable to the corporation as provided in division (A)(2) of this section if they vote for or assent to any of the following: (a) The payment of a dividend or distribution, the making of a distribution of assets to shareholders, or the purchase or redemption of the corporation's own shares, contrary in any such case to law or the articles; (b) A distribution of assets to shareholders during the winding up of the affairs of the corporation, on dissolution or otherwise, without the payment of all known obligations of the corporation or without making adequate provision for their payment; (c) The making of a loan, other than in the usual course of business, to an officer, director, or shareholder of the corporation, other than in either of the following cases: (i) In the case of a savings and loan association or of a corporation engaged in banking or in the making of loans generally; (ii) At the time of the making of the loan, a majority of the disinterested directors of the corporation voted for the loan and, taking into account the terms and provisions of the loan and other relevant factors, determined that the making of the loan could reasonably be expected to benefit the corporation. perturbation.justification: The addition of "believed to be" significantly weakens the representation and warranty provided by Global Energy. While Ohio Revised Code § 1701.95 primarily addresses liability for unlawful loans, dividends, and asset distribution, the Law Explanation suggests that the altered language undermines the intended diligence and accurate disclosure expected of corporate officers in broader contexts, potentially contradicting the spirit, if not the letter, of regulations requiring accurate representations. The original language provides a direct assertion of truth and correctness, whereas the modified language introduces a subjective element, allowing for a defense based on belief, even if unfounded. This shift creates an ambiguity that could hinder enforcement and contradicts the goal of ensuring accountability for representations within the contract.
